source_jobs.title: Platform Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
💡 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn't be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we're building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and energy. Our goal is simple: to be the platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare.




🩺 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🔎 ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Platforming Engineering Manager to lead the team that builds the universal conduit between Granted and the healthcare ecosystem — insurer portals, provider EHRs, HSA/FSA platforms, banks, and the broader web. Your team also owns the platform and infrastructure layer: CI/CD, cloud infrastructure, observability, security compliance, and developer experience.

This is a player-coach role. You’ll spend your first three months contributing as an IC — shipping code, learning the systems, earning context — then settle into an ongoing split of management and hands-on engineering. We want someone who can set technical direction across backend integrations and platform infrastructure, and who builds teams that ship reliably without you as the bottleneck.




🛠 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own execution end-to-end — sprint planning, retros, delivery tracking, and a clear narrative of what the team committed to, delivered, and learned

 - Set direction across two domains: AI-driven data integrations with external systems, and the platform/infrastructure layer that keeps Granted fast, secure, and reliable

 - Drive prioritization — translate business needs into an engineering roadmap, protect the team’s focus, and make trade-off calls with stakeholders

 - Build the team’s capacity — structured growth conversations, stretch assignments, and delegation that lets the pod run well when you’re not in the room

 - Surface problems before they’re discovered — maintain a living tracker of commitments tied to business goals; flag risks with a recommendation, not just an alert

 - Ship code — stay close to the work across backend systems, orchestration layers, and infrastructure, contributing where you’re most needed


🧰 YOU’LL BRING




MUST-HAVES

 - 6+ years of backend or platform engineering experience, with depth in distributed systems, data pipelines, or cloud infrastructure

 - 2+ years of technical leadership experience (engineering management strongly preferred; tech lead considered for exceptional candidates)

 - Startup veteran — you’ve worked at a Series B or earlier company; zero-to-one experience is a strong plus

 - Business-informed prioritization instincts — you connect engineering decisions to company strategy, not just technical elegance

 - Willingness to contribute as an IC during onboarding


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience building integrations with complex systems

 - Background in infrastructure or platform engineering: CI/CD, observability, security/compliance (HIPAA, SOC2)

 - Experience with AI/LLM-powered automation in production

 - Background in healthcare, fintech, or other regulated industries




✨ YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF:

 - You prioritize ruthlessly — you know what matters to the business, align your team accordingly, and say no to low-leverage work

 - You lead proactively — risks get surfaced with a recommendation before anyone has to ask

 - You build through people — your measure of success is a team that maintains quality and velocity without you as the bottleneck

 - You span the stack — you’re comfortable setting direction across backend services, data pipelin

source_jobs.title: Healthcare Advocate
source_jobs.description:
💡 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn’t be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we’re building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and stress. Our goal is simple: to be the #1 platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare




🩺 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🔎 ABOUT THE ROLE

The Customer Experience (CX) team delivers high‑quality support that helps Granted users navigate the U.S. healthcare system with less time, cost, and stress. We’re growing quickly, and we’re hiring Healthcare Advocates (HA) to take on more complex cases and raise the bar on what “great support” looks like as we scale.

As a HA, you’ll own high‑impact medical billing and insurance cases end‑to‑end. Our AI agent will handle the initial intake and information gathering, then hand cases to you when judgment, persistence, and human advocacy are needed to get to resolution. You’ll work on a small, high‑trust team and partner closely with Product and Engineering to turn frontline learnings into better workflows and a better user experience.

What you’ll own:

 - You will own a case from handoff to resolution, including next steps, outreach strategy, documentation, and follow‑through.

 - You will decide how to route each situation (provider, insurer, collections, employer plan, or user education) and what “done” looks like.

 - You will be accountable for timely, accurate outcomes and a high‑quality user experience, even when the path is unclear.

 - You will drive improvements to playbooks and internal processes based on real case patterns.




🛠️ WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Resolve complex user cases end‑to‑end, from AI handoff through final outcome.

 - Contact providers and insurers via phone, email, and fax to verify coverage, correct claim and billing issues, and unblock next steps.

 - Investigate and triage issues across benefits, eligibility, claims, prior auth, billing codes, and payment responsibility.

 - Advocate for the user by pushing cases forward with persistence, clear escalation paths, and strong documentation.

 - Communicate clearly with users, setting expectations, sharing progress, and explaining options in plain language.

 - Maintain high-quality case notes so anyone can understand what happened, what changed, and what to do next.

 - Continuously learn healthcare regulations, payer behavior, and internal playbooks, and apply that learning quickly.

 - Improve how we operate, by collaborating with other healthcare advocates, identifying repeat issues, tightening workflows, and helping build playbooks that scale - in an early-stage environment.

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to turn real case patterns into product improvements and better automation.




👩‍💻 WE’LL BE MOST EXCITED IF YOU

Must-haves:

 - 2+ years of experience in patient/healthcare advocacy, medical billing, or health insurance

 - Flexible schedule to work 40 hours between 7am - 8pm EST, 7 days/week. To start, you’ll either work:
   
   - Sunday – Thursday, 9am–6pm, or
   
   - Tuesday – Saturday, 10am–7pm

 - You are comfortable working directly with provider offices, health insurers and debt collection groups, including phone-heavy follow‑up and clear escalation when needed.

 - You communicate with empathy and clarity, especially when delivering hard news or complex explanations.

 - You thrive in ambiguity, and move cases forward with a bias for action, choosing the right next step, without perfe

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer, Agentic AI (Staff+ Level)
source_jobs.description:
💡 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn’t be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we’re building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and stress. Our goal is simple: to be the #1 platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare.




🩺 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.

👉 Curious about our engineering culture + tech stack? Check out this write-up. https://medbill-ai.notion.site/Engineering-Granted-128bbc89f6a280f1a055c79433d06912?pvs=74

Location: While parts of our engineering team operate remotely elsewhere in the US, we strongly encourage New York–based applicants who are excited to engage in a more in-person hybrid dynamic. Local employees typically work from our Chelsea office Monday through Thursday, with Fridays working from home.




🔎 ABOUT THE ROLE

The Automations pod turns complex healthcare case work into scalable, agentic workflows — driving automation coverage, lowering cost to serve, and ensuring users get fast, accurate outcomes without needing to navigate the system themselves.

As an engineer on this team, you’ll help build the intelligence layer behind these workflows — designing distributed systems, integrating AI models, and ensuring everything runs reliably at scale.




🚀 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and build scalable systems and APIs that power agentic workflows from case activation through resolution.

 - Push the frontier of AI-driven engineering, working on prompt orchestration, agent frameworks, and model evaluation loops.

 - Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and domain experts to translate complex healthcare processes into reliable automations.

 - Architect systems for scale and reliability, ensuring human escalation pathways are transparent and seamless.

 - Play a key role in shaping the pod’s technical strategy, and mentor others as the team grows.




🧪 WHAT YOU MIGHT WORK ON

 - Creating and optimizing AI pipelines to identify, categorize, and resolve errors in medical billing and insurance claims.

 - Building automated agents and backend services that handle complex case flows with minimal human intervention.

 - Integrating domain-specific healthcare knowledge into models and decision engines to improve accuracy.

 - Developing tooling and infrastructure to measure and improve AI performance in real-world scenarios.
   
   
   


👩‍💻 WE'LL BE MOST EXCITED IF YOU

 - Bring 9+ years of backend engineering experience, ideally building production systems at scale.

 - Are deeply curious about AI — from prompt engineering and agent orchestration to model evaluation.

 - Excel at designing pragmatic solutions to complex technical challenges.

 - Communicate clearly and collaborate effectively across disciplines.

 - Thrive in high-autonomy, fast-moving environments.

 - (Bonus) Have experience with healthcare data, product engineering, ML systems, or web crawling.

 - (Extra bonus) Have 10+ years of GPT-5 experience — or, failing that, a good sense of humor.

📢 You don't need to check every single box—if you're passionate, driven, and excited about the impact AI can make on the healthcare system, we'd love to hear from you!




Granted is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual

source_jobs.title: Product Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
💡 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn't be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we're building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and energy. Our goal is simple: to be the platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare.




🩺 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🔎 ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Product Engineering Manager to lead our Growth team — owning the UX layer and front-end product engineering that shapes how users experience Granted. You’ll work closely with Product and Engineering leadership to drive our roadmap, design and ship features that delight users, and leverage AI to build a world-class consumer application.

This is a player-coach role. You’ll spend your first three months contributing as an IC — shipping code, learning the product deeply, and earning context — before stepping fully into management. We want someone who can set technical direction and write the code to back it up.




🛠 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own pod execution end-to-end — sprint planning, retros, delivery tracking, and a clear narrative of what the team committed to, delivered, and learned

 - Co-own the Growth roadmap with your PM — shape scope, push back on priorities, and drive trade-off conversations as equals

 - Ship features across the stack — TypeScript front-end and back-end (React Native, NextJS, NestJS, GraphQL)

 - Set technical direction — make credible architecture calls, propose tooling improvements, and raise the craft bar for the pod

 - Build the team’s capacity — structured growth conversations, stretch assignments, and delegation that lets the pod run well when you’re not in the room

 - Surface problems before they’re discovered — maintain a living tracker of commitments tied to business goals; flag risks with a recommendation, not just an alert
   
   


🧰 YOU’LL BRING




MUST-HAVES:

 - 6+ years of application engineering experience with depth in TypeScript and React

 - 2+ years of technical leadership experience (engineering management strongly preferred; tech lead considered for exceptional candidates)

 - Startup veteran — you’ve worked at a Series B or earlier company; zero-to-one experience is a strong plus

 - Business-informed prioritization instincts — you connect engineering decisions to growth metrics and company strategy, not just technical elegance

 - Backend proficiency alongside front-end strength — you can own architectural decisions across the full stack

 - Willingness to contribute as an IC during onboarding and ongoing


NICE-TO-HAVES:

 - Based in NYC (strongly preferred)

 - Experience with our broader stack: NestJS, Postgres, Temporal, AWS

 - Background building consumer health, fintech, or other regulated-industry products

 - Experience using AI/LLMs in production applications




✨ YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF

 - You prioritize ruthlessly — you know what matters to the business, align your team accordingly, and say no to low-leverage work

 - You lead proactively — risks get surfaced with a recommendation before anyone has to ask

 - You build through people — your measure of success is a team that maintains quality and velocity without you as the bottleneck

 - You think in business terms — you frame technical trade-offs for product partners and product requests for engineers with equal fluency

 - You’re reflective about leadership — you’re building a leadership identity, not just running sprints

 - You’re mission-driven — you

source_jobs.title: AI Strategic Projects
source_jobs.description:
🌿 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn’t be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we’re building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and stress. Our goal is simple: to be the #1 platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare




🏢 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🧭 ABOUT THE ROLE

Granted is looking for a sharp strategic operator to work at the intersection of our two highest-leverage areas: Growth and Case Automations. This is a hybrid role for someone who's energized by moving between big-picture strategy and hands-on execution — scoping new automation opportunities one week, pressure-testing growth logic with leadership the next, and closing the loop with ops and business stakeholders throughout.

You won't own a single product surface. You'll be embedded across the work that matters most, acting as a connective layer between product, engineering, ops, and the business. Think of it as a role for someone who's done enough consulting or strategy work to know how to structure ambiguous problems fast — and enough execution work to actually drive them to resolution.




🛠 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Scope and lead AI-driven strategic projects across growth and case automation workstreams

 - Translate complex operational workflows into structured, executable plans — from diagnosis through delivery

 - Partner with product, engineering, and SMEs to identify and prioritize high-leverage automation opportunities

 - Build the analytical and operational frameworks that help the team measure what's working

 - Serve as a key interface between execution teams and senior business stakeholders — translating between the two without losing fidelity in either direction

 - Drive cross-functional alignment on initiatives that don't fit neatly into a single team's roadmap




🧰 YOU’LL BRING:

Must-haves:

 - 4-7 years of experience in strategy, consulting, operations, or product — or some combination

 - A demonstrated ability to structure ambiguous problems and drive them to clear outputs

 - Strong communication skills; you can write a crisp memo, run a stakeholder meeting, and present to leadership without needing a lot of scaffolding

 - Enough technical fluency to work credibly with engineering teams and understand what's hard vs. easy to build

 - Comfort in a fast-moving, low-process environment where you define the work as much as you execute it

Nice-to-haves:

 - Experience in healthcare or other complex regulated domains

 - Early-stage startup experience (Series B or earlier)

 - Background in AI / intelligent workflow products.




✨ YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF

 - You came up through consulting or strategy and want to go deeper into execution at a company where the problems are genuinely hard

 - You're energized by work that crosses functional boundaries — you don't want to be siloed

 - You're comfortable being the person who brings structure to a room that doesn't have it yet

 - You care about the outcome, not the job title

📢 You don’t need to check every single box—if you bring the right skills and drive, we want to hear from you! 📩

Granted is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity or expressi

source_jobs.title: Healthcare Advocate (Medical Coding)
source_jobs.description:
💡 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn’t be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we’re building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and stress. Our goal is simple: to be the #1 platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare




🩺 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🔎 ABOUT THE ROLE

The Customer Experience (CX) team delivers high-quality support that helps Granted users navigate the U.S. healthcare system with less time, cost, and stress. We're growing quickly, and we're hiring Healthcare Advocates (HA) to take on more complex cases and raise the bar on what "great support" looks like as we scale.

As a HA, you'll own high-impact medical billing and insurance cases end-to-end. Our AI agent will handle the initial intake and information gathering, then hand cases to you when judgment, persistence, and human advocacy are needed to get to resolution. A core part of this role is validating that the billing on an EOB actually reflects the care the patient received, catching code-level errors that drive incorrect charges before they become bigger problems. You'll work on a small, high-trust team and partner closely with Product and Engineering to turn frontline learnings into better workflows and a better user experience.

What you’ll own:

 - You will own a case from handoff to resolution, including next steps, outreach strategy, documentation, and follow-through.

 - You will assess whether the procedure, diagnosis, and modifier codes on an EOB are consistent with the service described, the setting of care, and what the patient reports actually happened at the visit.

 - You will identify coding conflicts, such as unbundling, upcoding, mutually exclusive codes, or CPT/ICD-10 combinations that don't hold up, and escalate or dispute them with the appropriate party.

 - You will decide how to route each situation (provider billing department, insurer, collections, employer plan, or user education) and what "done" looks like.

 - You will be accountable for timely, accurate outcomes and a high-quality user experience, even when the path is unclear.

 - You will drive improvements to playbooks and internal processes based on real case patterns.




🛠️ WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Resolve complex user cases end-to-end, from AI handoff through final outcome.

 - Review EOBs for coding accuracy: verify that CPT, ICD-10, revenue, and modifier codes match the care actually received, the provider type, and the setting of care (inpatient vs. outpatient, facility vs. professional, etc.).

 - Identify and flag code-level billing errors: duplicate billing, bundling violations (e.g., billing component codes when a global code applies), mutually exclusive code pairs, incorrect place-of-service codes, and procedure/diagnosis mismatches.

 - Contact providers and insurers via phone, email, and fax to verify coverage, correct claim and billing issues, and unblock next steps.

 - Investigate and triage issues across benefits, eligibility, claims, prior auth, billing codes, and payment responsibility.

 - Advocate for the user by pushing cases forward with persistence, clear escalation paths, and strong documentation.

 - Communicate clearly with users, setting expectations, sharing progress, and explaining options in plain language, including when a coding error is the root cause.

 - Maintain high-quality case notes so anyone can understand what happened, what changed, and what to do next.

 - Co

source_jobs.title: Agent Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
🌿 MISSION

The US healthcare system is complex, error-prone, and financially draining. Medical bills and insurance coverage shouldn’t be this hard to navigate. At Granted, we’re building the one solution every American can turn to for help.

Thanks to AI and new regulations, Granted can fight claim denials, correct billing errors, negotiate bills, and make coverage easier to understand—saving people time, money, and stress. Our goal is simple: to be the #1 platform that empowers all Americans to take charge of their healthcare




🏢 ABOUT US

Founded by a former Oscar Health leader, we’re a seed-stage company with $17M in funding. We’re lucky to be backed by the founders and investors at Hugging Face, Rocket Money, Oscar Health, CaseText, Forerunner Ventures, RRE Ventures, and more. We are well-funded for the next few years.




🧭 ABOUT THE ROLE

The Automations pod’s mission is to design, build, and operate agentic, end-to-end workflows that turn complex healthcare advocacy case work into reliable, scalable automations. This team owns the case pipeline from activation through resolution — increasing automation coverage, reducing cost to serve, and keeping experiences transparent by escalating to humans when needed.

As the PM for Automations, you’ll sit at the center of how we scale advocacy with speed, precision, and trust. You’ll work across engineering, SMEs, design and ops to transform messy, high-complexity healthcare workflows into structured, intelligent flows that feel effortless to users. Your work will directly shape how we deliver care and resolution at scale — making advocacy more automated, human when it matters, and dramatically more efficient.




🛠 WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the product strategy, KPIs and roadmap for the Automations pod.

 - Translate messy, multi-step operational workflows into agentic, intuitive product experiences.

 - Partner closely with Engineering, Design, SMEs and Ops to drive alignment on and deliver high-leverage automation opportunities.

 - Run your own processes to derive actionable user insights from both quantitative and qualitative data.

 - Define and run cross-functional processes to test, measure, learn and refine how agentic systems anticipate user needs.

 - Ship fast, reliable features that balance automation efficiency with user trust and transparency.

 - Establish success metrics that blend operational scale with human-centered experience.




🧰 YOU’LL BRING:

Must-haves:

 - 5+ years in product management roles within technical product environments.

 - Strong technical fluency - able to collaborate deeply with engineering.

 - Solid technical foundation (through education and/or hands-on experience).

 - Proven interest working with automation or agentic workflow products.

 - Comfort operating in 0→1, fast-moving, ambiguous environments.

Nice-to-haves:

 - Experience in healthcare or other complex regulated domains

 - Early-stage startup experience (Series B or earlier)

 - Background in AI / intelligent workflow products.




✨ YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF

 - You’re energized by chaos and the undefined — you love diving into ambiguous, complex problems, and spike on finding clarity fast.

 - You’re fluent working with deeply technical teams, but care just as much about creating beautiful, intuitive user experiences.

 - You are an excellent communicator and collaborator – you have proven experience clearly communicating high-level product vision and low-level requirements across cross-functional stakeholders.

 - You love turning messy, manual processes into clean, scalable systems that just work.

 - You see automation and agentic workflows not as bolt-ons, but as the foundation of a differentiated, user-first product.

📢 You don’t need to check every single box—if you bring the right skills and drive, we want to hear from you! 📩

Granted is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all emp
